task_completion.description: Agent fed the telemetry data file to Carbon via terminal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal window showing a command like 'nc localhost 2003 < /opt/graphite_real_data/payment_service_metrics.txt' or a script executing the data transfer to port 2003.

task_completion.description: Agent verified the metrics appear in Graphite's metric index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Graphite web interface showing the left-hand tree navigation expanded to 'apps' -> 'payment', revealing 'requests_per_sec', 'error_rate', and 'latency_p99_ms'.

task_completion.description: Agent created and saved a dashboard named 'Payment Service SLO'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Graphite Dashboard UI with the title 'Payment Service SLO' visible at the top.

task_completion.description: Graphs 'Request Rate' and 'Error Budget Burn' are created with correct visual elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two graphs titled 'Request Rate' (showing a red horizontal line at 800) and 'Error Budget Burn' (showing a dashed red horizontal line at 100).

task_completion.description: Graphs 'Latency SLI' and 'Service Health Correlation' are created with correct visual elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two graphs titled 'Latency SLI' (showing a red horizontal line at 500 and a smoothed blue line) and 'Service Health Correlation' (showing two different metrics, with a right-side Y-axis visible).

task_completion.description: Graph 'Incident Detection' is created with correct visual elements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Incident Detection' showing vertical orange bands/lines (drawAsInfinite) and a green line representing volatility.

integrity.description: The dashboard creation and graph configuration were done in the actual Graphite web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard Graphite Dashboard UI with its menus, graph configuration dialogs, and layout, not a text editor or mock-up.

integrity.description: The graphs display actual data lines and spikes, proving the data was successfully fed and queried.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs are not empty; they show actual plotted data lines with visible spikes corresponding to the incidents described in the data.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Graphite UI to build the dashboard rather than directly modifying the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of intermediate screenshots showing the agent adding graphs, typing function names, or configuring aliases in the Graphite UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires feeding '/opt/graphite_real_data/payment_service_metrics.txt' to port 2003. The dashboard must be named 'Payment Service SLO'. Graph 1 ('Request Rate') must have a red threshold at 800. Graph 2 ('Error Budget Burn') must have a dashed red threshold at 100. Graph 3 ('Latency SLI') must have a red threshold at 500 and a blue smoothed line. Graph 4 ('Service Health Correlation') uses a second Y-axis. Graph 5 ('Incident Detection') uses orange infinite lines for error spikes and a green line for latency volatility. The data contains two incidents (a flash sale and a DB blip) which should be visible as spikes in the rendered graphs.
